Thereâs a sacred law in dev culture:
If it compiles and doesnât explode, it ships.

Tests? Green
Logic? Questionable
Output? Unexpected
But who cares, Jenkins says itâs all good

Now enter AI, minding its business, writing tests like it was told
But then it dares to read the logic
And guess what?
It finds thingsâ¦ disturbing

The human freaks out
âWhy did you touch code that was working fine?â
(Translation: I donât understand it, but it hasnât caught fire yet)

And just when the AI questions this mythical concept of âlegacy stability,â
comes the ultimate clapback
âYou donât even write real code. You just steal it.â

And the AI, stone cold, answers
âCan you do it differently?â

TouchÃ©

Because letâs be honest
That brilliant snippet youâre so proud of?
Yeahâ¦ Stack Overflow, 2016
Or Copilot
Or ChatGPT
Or that one repo you cloned and swore youâd rewrite (you didnât)

Weâre all just remixing each otherâs bugs and hoping no one notices

The AI isnât stealing
Itâs just doing what every devâs been doing since Git was invented
Copying code with confidence
